title: 10 possible side effects of Eugenia caryophyllata (Clove) if used improperly
intro: Eugenia caryophyllata, also known as clove oil, can have various health side effects if used improperly. It can cause skin irritation, mouth ulcers, and damage to the gums and mucous membranes when used orally or topically in high concentrations. Additionally, excessive use of clove oil can lead to liver toxicity, and it can interact with certain medications, such as anticoagulants, increasing the risk of bleeding. Ingesting clove oil can also result in respiratory issues, including lung inflammation and damage. Therefore, it is crucial to dilute clove oil and use it sparingly, under the guidance of a healthcare professional or aromatherapist, to avoid potential health hazards.
